text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-tooltip repository at the commit where the bugs below reproduce. Find and fix the bugs in the library source.

Bug 1 (touch devices): react-tooltip supports a `touch` open behavior alongside mouse/focus hover, but on touch-only devices tapping an anchor never opens the tooltip at all when the tooltip is configured to open on hover-style events (mouseenter/mouseover/focus). The component sets up its global, delegated event listeners once per anchor-selector configuration, and this setup path only attaches handlers for the mouse/focus events it's configured to open on — there is no corresponding `touchstart` handler wired into that same delegated listener setup, so a tap produces no `mouseenter`/`mouseover`/`focus` and the tooltip never shows.

Bug 2 (reappearing after scroll/resize close): a tooltip can be configured to close automatically when the page scrolls or the window resizes. If the user opens the tooltip (which schedules the tooltip to show after a configurable show-delay) and a scroll or resize event fires while that show is still pending — i.e. before the delayed show has actually applied — the scroll/resize handler hides the tooltip, but the previously scheduled show is not cancelled. That pending show still fires afterward and makes the tooltip reappear even though the user's scroll/resize should have kept it closed. Expected: once scroll or resize closes a tooltip, no show that was already in flight before that close should be allowed to reopen it after the fact.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (Node, yarn classic v1). You can scope the existing Jest suite to whatever file you're iterating on with `npx jest <path-to-file>`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

message: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.

text: I’ll trace the delegated event setup and the delayed show/scroll-resize close path, then add focused regression coverage alongside the source fixes. I’ll also check prior Nowledge context for any repository-specific decisions before concluding.

      "title": "react-tooltip anchor transition and delayShow fix",
      "content": "react-tooltip delegated mouseout/focusout handlers now resolve both event.target and relatedTarget through the configured anchor resolver, so transitions between different anchors sharing one tooltip remain active even before activeAnchor updates. Tooltip delayed-show uses a renderedRef updated each render instead of a captured rendered value, preventing stale delay bypass decisions.",

    },
    {
      "id": "b87bbe62-cf3e-4973-965a-4ec3224806e4",
      "title": "Clickable tooltip hover suppresses delayed close",
      "content": "In react-tooltip clickable mode, the tooltip must track direct mouseenter/mouseleave on its own element and cancel the pending hide timer on mouseenter. The anchor schedules a delayed hide when left; only setting a hovering ref was timing-sensitive and could allow the close timer to win before interactive content was reachable. Regression coverage advances the 100ms delay and asserts the react-tooltip__show class.",
      "score": 0.8514999999999999,
      "importance": 0.8,
      "unit_type": "learning",
      "labels": [
        "label_react-tooltip"

    },

      "id": "a1b412a6-4b92-4031-bc14-64ebb1b5fdac",
      "title": "react-tooltip delegated events capture stopPropagation",
      "content": "react-tooltip shared delegated anchor listeners must be registered in the capture phase. Registering document listeners with capture=true lets openOnClick and other delegated anchor events run before an anchor handler can stopPropagation; cleanup and reset must pass the same capture flag.",
      "score": 0.66579920113691,
      "importance": 0.8,

      "labels": [

        "label_event-delegation"

      "space_id": "default"
    },
    {
      "id": "31389db3-21a2-484e-8c43-44f0c3d55b36",
      "title": "react-tooltip ignores non-element delegated event targets",
      "content": "react-tooltip delegated anchor resolution now returns null before any element-only access when EventTarget is not an Element (for example document). This makes delegated hover, focus, and click handlers safely ignore non-element targets without attaching or closing a tooltip, while preserving existing connected-element resolution. Fix is in src/components/Tooltip/use-tooltip-events.tsx and focused interaction/anchor-selection Jest suites plus TypeScript and ESLint checks pass.",
      "score": 0.6250548050872116,

    {
      "id": "e4fae2ba-9738-42af-80e7-e9a191604a67",
      "title": "react-datepicker multi-month custom header navigation reset",
      "content": "In react-datepicker, day selection stores the visible panel index in DatePicker state.monthSelectedIn, which Calendar uses to offset the rendered months. A custom header's changeMonth callback changes Calendar's internal date but does not clear that index. The fix is to adapt renderCustomHeader in DatePicker and reset monthSelectedIn to 0 before delegating to the Calendar callback, preserving panel-origin behavior for day selection while making custom month jumps start at the leftmost panel. Regression coverage belongs in the DatePicker test suite with monthsShown={2}, a day click in the second month, and a custom-header changeMonth assertion.",

import type { ElementType, CSSProperties, RefObject, ReactNode } from 'react'

export type PlacesType =
  | 'top'
  | 'top-start'
  | 'top-end'
  | 'right'
  | 'right-start'
  | 'right-end'
  | 'bottom'
  | 'bottom-start'
  | 'bottom-end'
  | 'left'
  | 'left-start'
  | 'left-end'

export type VariantType = 'dark' | 'light' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'info'

export type WrapperType = ElementType | 'div' | 'span'

export type PositionStrategy = 'absolute' | 'fixed'

export type DataAttribute =
  | 'place'
  | 'content'
  | 'variant'
  | 'offset'
  | 'wrapper'
  | 'position-strategy'
  | 'delay-show'
  | 'delay-hide'
  | 'auto-close'
  | 'float'
  | 'hidden'
  | 'class-name'

/**
 * @description floating-ui middleware
 */
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
export type Middleware = any

export interface IPosition {
  x: number
  y: number
}

export interface TooltipImperativeOpenOptions {
  anchorSelect?: string
  position?: IPosition
  place?: PlacesType
  content?: ReactNode
  /**
   * @description Delay (in ms) before opening the tooltip.
   */
  delay?: number
}

export interface TooltipImperativeCloseOptions {
  /**
   * @description Delay (in ms) before closing the tooltip.
   */
  delay?: number
}

export interface TooltipRefProps {
  open: (options?: TooltipImperativeOpenOptions) => void
  close: (options?: TooltipImperativeCloseOptions) => void
  /**
   * @readonly
   */
  activeAnchor: Element | null
  /**
   * @readonly
   */
  place: PlacesType
  /**
   * @readonly
   */
  isOpen: boolean
}

type AnchorClickEvents = 'click' | 'dblclick' | 'mousedown' | 'mouseup'

export type AnchorOpenEvents = Partial<
  Record<'mouseenter' | 'focus' | 'mouseover' | AnchorClickEvents, boolean>
>
export type AnchorCloseEvents = Partial<
  Record<'mouseleave' | 'blur' | 'mouseout' | AnchorClickEvents, boolean>
>
export type GlobalCloseEvents = {
  escape?: boolean
  scroll?: boolean
  resize?: boolean
  clickOutsideAnchor?: boolean
}

export interface ITooltip {
  forwardRef?: React.ForwardedRef<TooltipRefProps>
  className?: string
  classNameArrow?: string
  content?: ReactNode
  contentWrapperRef?: RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
  portalRoot?: Element | null
  place?: PlacesType
  offset?: number
  id?: string
  variant?: VariantType
  anchorSelect?: string
  wrapper: WrapperType
  openOnClick?: boolean
  positionStrategy?: PositionStrategy
  middlewares?: Middleware[]
  delayShow?: number
  delayHide?: number
  autoClose?: number
  float?: boolean
  hidden?: boolean
  noArrow?: boolean
  clickable?: boolean
  openEvents?: AnchorOpenEvents
  closeEvents?: AnchorCloseEvents
  globalCloseEvents?: GlobalCloseEvents
  imperativeModeOnly?: boolean
  style?: CSSProperties
  position?: IPosition
  isOpen?: boolean
  defaultIsOpen?: boolean
  setIsOpen?: (value: boolean) => void
  afterShow?: () => void
  afterHide?: () => void
  disableTooltip?: (anchorRef: Element | null) => boolean
  previousActiveAnchor: Element | null
  activeAnchor: Element | null
  setActiveAnchor: (anchor: Element | null) => void
  border?: CSSProperties['border']
  opacity?: CSSProperties['opacity']
  arrowColor?: CSSProperties['backgroundColor']
  arrowSize?: number
  role?: React.AriaRole
}

text: The two failure points are localized: delegated anchor setup has no `touchstart` registration, and the global close callback only calls `handleShow(false)` without clearing `tooltipShowDelayTimerRef`. I’ll wire touch opening into the same hover-style activation path and clear the pending show timer at the moment scroll/resize closes the tooltip, then add tests for both timing-sensitive cases.

The source patch is in place. The touch listener is enabled only when the tooltip uses the normal hover/focus opening modes, so explicitly click-only configurations keep their existing semantics; the close callback now invalidates any delayed or deferred anchor-switch show before closing.

input.title: react-tooltip touchstart and scroll-delay race fixes
input.content: In react-tooltip, delegated anchor event setup must register a document-level touchstart opener whenever hover/focus-style opening is enabled, because touch-only browsers may emit neither mouseenter/mouseover nor focus. The global scroll/resize close handler must clear tooltipShowDelayTimerRef before closing so an in-flight delayed show cannot reopen the tooltip after the close. Regression tests belong in tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js.

text:
Fixed both bugs in [use-tooltip-events.tsx](/home/user/workspace/src/components/Tooltip/use-tooltip-events.tsx:328):

- Added delegated `touchstart` opening for hover/focus tooltips.
- Cancelled pending show timers when scroll or resize closes the tooltip.

Added regression tests in [tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js](/home/user/workspace/src/test/tooltip-interaction-behavior.spec.js:92).

Validation passed: 48 Jest tests, TypeScript, and ESLint.
